article_title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
obligation_text: Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system interaction disclosure requirements, including design of notification mechanisms, assessment of when disclosure is obvious, and implementation of transparency measures for human-AI interaction.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system interaction disclosure requirements, including design of notification mechanisms, assessment of when disclosure is obvious, and implementation of transparency measures for human-AI interaction.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and their operationalization, including fairness metrics, explainability methods, human oversight requirements, and AI impact assessment methodologies.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 50(1) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-004 is the strongest match (STS: 0.745, cosine: 0.597). Secondary: AG-K-007 (AG, cosine: 0.581), SP-A-003 (SP, cosine: 0.572).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.5/10 (raw 0.745). Shared vocabulary: [ai, assessment, human, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [design, disclosure, implementation, interaction, measures, mechanisms] vs WIDAI terms [explainability, fairness, impact, methodologies, methods, metrics] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.899 (P=0.895, R=0.903). Balanced P/R — the 26-word competency implication and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (86.6%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.597 < STS 0.745 (gap -0.148). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning between [design, disclosure, implementation] and [explainability, fairness, impact] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 25.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, assessment, human, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act-only: [design, disclosure, implementation, interaction, measures, mechanisms]. WIDAI-only: [explainability, fairness, impact, methodologies, methods, metrics].
